body:
## Context
Track and close review findings from PR #1: https://github.com/monet88/mandarin-learn/pull/1

## Fix Order
P1 security/integrity issues first, then P2 correctness/UX.

### P1 (must fix before merge)
- [ ] #7 [HSK][Security] Prevent answer key exposure in exam question payloads
- [ ] #8 [HSK] Normalize generated question answer field end-to-end
- [ ] #9 [HSK][Security] Lock down hsk-refresh-question-bank write path
- [ ] #10 [HSK][Security] Require session ownership and quota checks in writing evaluator
- [ ] #11 [HSK] Enforce per-section deadlines and sequence server-side

### P2 (follow right after P1)
- [ ] #12 [HSK][Mobile] Replace dynamic require in vocab bundles with static imports
- [ ] #13 [HSK] Recompute progress after mastery updates in sync flow
- [ ] #14 [HSK] Make exam finalization idempotent to prevent duplicate results
- [ ] #15 [HSK][UX] Let first-time users start review directly from Browse
- [ ] #16 [HSK] Invalidate stale session state when refresh fails

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All linked issues closed with tests/regression checks
- [ ] `npm run lint` has no new errors (warnings acceptable)
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] PR #1 updated and re-reviewed

## Notes
- Existing positives from review remain valid: server-side exam-start quota/premium checks, RevenueCat webhook idempotency on `rc_event_id`, and TS typecheck passing.
title: [HSK][Tracking] Fix PR #1 review findings

body:
## Finding
`useHskSession` keeps stale session data when refresh fails, so premium/quota decisions can be made from old state.

## Affected Files
- `hooks/useHskSession.ts:32`
- `components/hsk/hsk-prep.tsx:42`
- `components/hsk/hsk-level.tsx:40`

## Proposed Fix
- Clear or mark session stale on refresh failure.
- Block gated actions until fresh session load succeeds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Failed refresh transitions session state to stale/invalid.
- Premium/quota-gated actions are blocked on stale state.
- UI shows recoverable reload path and revalidation behavior.
title: [HSK] Invalidate stale session state when refresh fails

body:
## Finding
First-time users cannot move from Browse into an actual review card because Review only shows preexisting due items.

## Affected Files
- `components/hsk/hsk-vocab-study.tsx:100`
- `components/hsk/hsk-vocab-study.tsx:127`
- `hooks/useHskReviewQueue.ts:37`

## Proposed Fix
- Seed tapped Browse word into first-review state, or
- Open a study/review card directly from Browse action.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New user can tap a Browse word and immediately enter a review/study card.
- Queue/state remains consistent after first review.
- Add UI flow test for first-time path.
title: [HSK][UX] Let first-time users start review directly from Browse

body:
## Finding
Exam finalization is not idempotent; auto-submit/manual-submit races can create duplicate results and overcount quota.

## Affected Files
- `supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-submit-section/index.ts:135`
- `supabase/migrations/20260310_hsk_core_tables.sql:87`
- `components/hsk/hsk-exam.tsx:143`

## Proposed Fix
- Add `UNIQUE(session_id)` on results/finalization table.
- Add FK constraints if missing.
- Make finalization transactional and idempotent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Concurrent submits produce a single finalized result.
- Quota is consumed once per session.
- Add race-condition test for double finalize attempts.
title: [HSK] Make exam finalization idempotent to prevent duplicate results

body:
## Finding
`hsk-sync-events` recomputes progress before applying incoming `word_reviewed` mastery updates, so progress lags one sync behind.

## Affected Files
- `supabase/functions/hsk-sync-events/index.ts:80`
- `supabase/functions/hsk-session-init/index.ts:41`
- `components/hsk/hsk-level.tsx:40`

## Proposed Fix
- Apply mastery updates first, then recompute aggregates in the same request/transaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Progress response reflects newly submitted review events immediately.
- No one-sync delay in server-authoritative progress.
- Add integration test for update + aggregate in one sync request.
title: [HSK] Recompute progress after mastery updates in sync flow

body:
## Finding
Dynamic JSON `require()` in `hsk-vocab-study.tsx` is not Metro-safe, causing potential runtime load failures.

## Affected File
- `components/hsk/hsk-vocab-study.tsx:44`

## Proposed Fix
- Replace dynamic require pattern with static import/bundle map for each HSK level.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Metro resolves all HSK word bundles statically.
- No runtime module resolution errors on iOS/Android.
- Add test or smoke check for bundle loading per level.
title: [HSK][Mobile] Replace dynamic require in vocab bundles with static imports

body:
## Finding
Section deadlines are currently client-only; backend enforces only global expiry.

## Affected File
- `supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-submit-section/index.ts:75`

## Risk
Late/out-of-order submits can still be accepted by API.

## Proposed Fix
- Persist `current_section` and section deadline timestamps in `hsk_exam_sessions`.
- Reject late section submits and out-of-order transitions on server.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Server rejects section submit after section deadline.
- Server rejects out-of-order section submission.
- Session progression updates atomically with submission.
title: [HSK] Enforce per-section deadlines and sequence server-side

body:
## Finding
`hsk-writing-evaluate` is callable by any authenticated user with arbitrary text and no session/quota/entitlement checks.

## Affected File
- `supabase/functions/hsk-writing-evaluate/index.ts:61`

## Risk
Abuse vector and quota bypass.

## Proposed Fix
- Require `session_id` input.
- Verify caller owns the referenced exam session.
- Enforce premium/quota/rate limits server-side.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Requests without valid owned `session_id` are rejected.
- Non-premium or out-of-quota users are blocked server-side.
- Endpoint includes rate limiting and tests for abuse paths.
title: [HSK][Security] Require session ownership and quota checks in writing evaluator

body:
## Finding
Any premium user can invoke `hsk-refresh-question-bank`, mutate shared question bank, and spend AI credits through a service-role write path.

## Affected File
- `supabase/functions/hsk-refresh-question-bank/index.ts:44`

## Risk
Unauthorized data mutation and unbounded cost exposure.

## Proposed Fix
- Restrict endpoint to admin/service callers only.
- Add server-side rate limiting.
- Add audit logging for caller identity and mutation count.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Non-admin premium users receive forbidden response.
- Admin/service path still works.
- Rate limiting is enforced and covered by tests.
title: [HSK][Security] Lock down hsk-refresh-question-bank write path

body:
## Finding
Generated questions use `correct_answer`, but scoring/review currently read `answer`, causing mismatched scoring and review.

## Affected Files
- `supabase/functions/hsk-refresh-question-bank/index.ts:168`
- `supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-submit-section/index.ts:23`
- `components/hsk/HskExamResults.tsx:98`

## Proposed Fix
- Pick one canonical field (`answer` or `correct_answer`) and normalize generation, persistence, scoring, and UI review paths.
- Add compatibility mapping for existing rows if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generated and static question payloads score correctly.
- Review UI shows the same canonical answer key used in scoring.
- Add generated-payload scoring test to prevent regressions.
title: [HSK] Normalize generated question answer field end-to-end

body:
## Finding
Answer keys are exposed to authenticated clients through `hsk_question_bank.question_data`.

## Affected Files
- `supabase/migrations/20260310_hsk_content_tables.sql:9`
- `supabase/migrations/20260310_hsk_content_tables.sql:24`
- `lib/hsk-exam.ts:65`

## Risk
Users can inspect payloads and see correct answers before submitting.

## Proposed Fix
- Serve exam questions via server path that strips `answer`/`correct_answer` before returning to clients.
- Or separate answer keys into a server-only store/table not readable by client role.
- Keep scoring on server only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Client-facing exam payload never contains answer keys.
- Server scoring still works with hidden answer data.
- Add regression test that asserts answer fields are absent from exam fetch response.
title: [HSK][Security] Prevent answer key exposure in exam question payloads

body:
## Summary
`app/hsk-exam.tsx` registers its AppState listener once, so the callback keeps the initial `screen` value captured at mount.

## Impact
After a long background interruption, the `screen === \"exam\"` guard can remain false and the auto-submit path never runs.

## Scope
- File: `app/hsk-exam.tsx`
- Related PR: #1

## Expected
The background interruption handler should read current exam state from refs or a stable callback so auto-submit works while an exam is in progress.

## Actual
The handler can use stale screen state and skip auto-submit.
title: Fix stale AppState closure in HSK exam background auto-submit

body:
## Summary
`lib/hsk-event-queue.ts` flushes offline review events into `hsk_review_events`, but this PR does not add any SQL migration creating that table.

## Impact
On a clean deploy, offline review sync will fail at runtime because the target table does not exist.

## Scope
- File: `lib/hsk-event-queue.ts`
- Missing schema: `hsk_review_events`
- Related PR: #1

## Expected
Either add the missing migration for `hsk_review_events` or change the client to use an existing supported ingest path.

## Actual
The client writes to a table that is absent from the checked-in migrations.
title: Add missing schema for offline HSK review sync

body:
## Summary
`supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-start/index.ts` blocks new exam creation whenever it finds an `active` session, but it never checks whether that session is already expired.

## Impact
A crashed or abandoned exam can leave a stale `active` row behind and permanently block the user from starting a new exam.

## Scope
- File: `supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-start/index.ts`
- Related PR: #1

## Expected
The start flow should ignore, expire, or clean up stale sessions whose `expires_at` is already in the past.

## Actual
Any row with `status = 'active'` blocks new sessions indefinitely.
title: Handle stale active exam sessions before blocking new starts

body:
## Summary
`supabase/functions/revenuecat-webhook/index.ts` treats RevenueCat `CANCELLATION` as an immediate revoke and mirrors that into `profiles.is_premium = false`.

## Impact
Users lose premium access as soon as they turn off auto-renew, even though their subscription should remain active until the paid period actually expires.

## Scope
- File: `supabase/functions/revenuecat-webhook/index.ts`
- Related PR: #1

## Expected
`CANCELLATION` should keep access active until an actual expiration/revocation event occurs.

## Actual
The webhook flips the profile to non-premium immediately on cancellation.
title: Do not revoke premium on RevenueCat CANCELLATION before expiration

body:
## Summary
`supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-start/index.ts` validates auth and `hsk_level`, then creates an exam session immediately. It never enforces premium access or the free daily exam quota server-side.

## Impact
Any authenticated user can call the function directly and start unlimited exams, including premium-only levels.

## Scope
- File: `supabase/functions/hsk-mock-exam-start/index.ts`
- Related PR: #1

## Expected
The function should verify premium entitlement and free-tier daily quota before inserting a new exam session.

## Actual
Session creation only checks auth, input validity, and whether another active session exists.
title: Enforce premium and daily exam quota in hsk-mock-exam-start
